task_response.descriptor_evidence: 01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5 (incompletely addressed; position visible but underdeveloped; some irrelevant detail)

coherence.descriptor_evidence: 01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5 (organisation evident but not wholly logical; lack of overall progression; reference inaccurate)
coherence.justification: Three paragraphs are visible, but progression breaks down within Body 1, which jumps from Mesopotamia to "*People wanted to deliver their messages only*" to Morse to computers without explicit linking. References are repeatedly unclear: "*Personally, and especially, that shone*" (what is *that*?), "*By doing that, it can calculate*" (what is the antecedent of *that*?). Repetition of "*People wanted ... People wanted ... people finally invented*" is the mechanical pattern flagged in `05-band-6-to-7/cohesion-upgrades.md` §2. The truncation removes any inter-paragraph synthesis.

lexical.descriptor_evidence: 01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 6 (generally adequate; restricted range; lacks precision in word choice)
lexical.justification: Topic-relevant items are present — "*electronic signals*", "*binary digits*", "*hardware*", "*base-60*", "*switches*". But word-choice slips are dense for one paragraph: "*new logics*" (uncountable in this sense), "*mathematics is inevitable*" (wrong collocation; use *indispensable / essential*), "*the first ever information which only used electronic signals*" (information ≠ message), "*a single hardware*" (uncountable), "*go up to almost countless numbers*" (vague). This is the textbook Band 6 profile in `01-band-descriptors/band-6-vs-7-comparison.md`: meaning is generally clear but range is restricted and precision lapses.

grammar.descriptor_evidence: 01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5 (complex sentences attempted but faulty; simple sentences most accurate)
grammar.justification: Several attempts at complex structures break: "*Computers consist of a full of small switches*" (garbled), "*so that no physical objects such as people or dove needed*" (missing *were*; *dove* uncountable/wrong plural), "*not to mention the Babylonians used base-60*" (clause after *not to mention* — a Korean-L1 transfer pattern noted in `09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md` §E.3). Simple sentences ("*Computers consist of …*", "*The graphic on the screen is just a number as well*") are mostly accurate, but error-free sentences are not frequent — the gating Band 7 criterion in `01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 7.

overall_rationale: The four criterion scores average 5.25, which rounds up to **5.5** under `01-band-descriptors/scoring-rules.md` §3. Lexical resource alone reaches Band 6; truncation, position drift, and broken complex sentences hold the other three criteria at Band 5.
what_changed: The prior single-shot feedback was substantively right on the band profile and on the structural diagnosis (truncation, drifting body 1, missing conclusion). I keep the band scores and most of the rewrites but sharpen the calibration: this essay sits **below** the band-6 anchor `04-task2-essays/samples-band-6_0.md` §Sample 1 (Wages of Entertainers, ~290 words **with** a real conclusion) because the response here lacks a conclusion and Body 1 drifts to communication history rather than the mathematics→computing causal chain. I also add: (i) a concession-pattern rewrite that lets the candidate take a defensible position on the "most important driver" claim, (ii) a flag on the L1-Korean "not to mention + clause" pattern from `09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md`, and (iii) a cleft-sentence upgrade for the binary topic sentence drawn from `09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md` §5.

rewrites.original: In this essay, I will discuss how mathematics influenced computer science.
rewrites.improved: Important as engineering and physics have been to computing, this essay will argue that mathematics — through formal logic, binary representation and algorithms — has been the single most decisive driver of the computer revolution.
rewrites.reason: Adds a clear position, a concession, and a three-point roadmap; one `Adj + as + S + V` showcase.

rewrites.original: (essay ends at "…just a number as well." with no conclusion)
rewrites.improved: Taken together, these examples show that mathematics provided the very language in which information is stored, transmitted and processed; without it, the hardware breakthroughs of the twentieth century would have remained inert engineering.
rewrites.reason: Supplies the missing synthesis conclusion and re-states position — the single biggest TR lift.

rewrites.original: People have built up mathematical rules by defining new logics to solve problems.
rewrites.improved: Over millennia, mathematicians have built systems of formal logic that allow problems to be defined precisely and resolved step by step.
rewrites.reason: Fixes "*new logics*" (uncountable misuse) and names the mechanism (formal logic) the rest of the essay actually depends on.

rewrites.original: Computers consist of a full of small switches which can turn on and off.
rewrites.improved: What underpins every modern computer is, in essence, an array of tiny switches that can be either on or off.
rewrites.reason: Replaces broken "*a full of*" with a cleft topic sentence — one band-7 GRA showcase in the strongest paragraph.

rewrites.original: so that no physical objects such as people or dove needed
rewrites.improved: so that messages no longer had to be carried by people or carrier pigeons
rewrites.reason: Fixes missing passive *were*, countability of *dove*, and word choice.

rewrites.original: If it has billions and trillions of switches, then it can go up to almost countless numbers.
rewrites.improved: Were a machine to contain billions of such switches, it could represent numbers and data of essentially unbounded scale.
rewrites.reason: Replaces vague causality with an inverted second conditional — high-yield GRA showcase.

rewrites.original: the first ever information which only used electronic signals, so that no physical objects such as people or dove needed
rewrites.improved: one of the earliest systems for transmitting information by electrical signal alone, dispensing with physical couriers altogether
rewrites.reason: Corrects "*first ever information*" (information ≠ message) and replaces a relative-clause stack with a participle clause.
Fixes a wrong-collocation (*inevitable* describes events, not tools).

rewrites.original: not to mention the Babylonians used base-60 for timekeeping
rewrites.improved: the Babylonians, for instance, employed a base-60 numeral system that still shapes how we keep time today
rewrites.reason: Removes the L1-Korean "*not to mention + clause*" calque and integrates the example into the argument.

rewrites.original: a machine which can do several things in a single hardware, a computer.
rewrites.improved: a single piece of hardware capable of performing many different operations — what we now call a computer.
rewrites.reason: Fixes the uncountable error "*a single hardware*" and tightens the apposition.

convergence_note: The prior feedback chain shown to me is empty, so per the harness rules this must be an early-iteration `REFINING`. There is also still substantive room to push: I added a concession-pattern thesis, an L1-Korean "*not to mention*" diagnosis, and two fresh band-7 grammar showcases (cleft, inverted conditional) that the prior did not contain.
raw_markdown:
# Iteration 02 — claude

_Generated 2026-05-10T10:06:29Z. Prior chain depth: up to 3. Manifest: 462 files._

---I have sufficient corpus context. Producing the feedback now. Since the prior chain block in the prompt is empty, this is an early iteration and I'll mark REFINING per the rules.

---

## Per-criterion scores
| Criterion | Band | Descriptor evidence (file path) |
|---|---|---|
| Task Response                | 5.0 | `01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5 (incompletely addressed; position visible but underdeveloped; some irrelevant detail) |
| Coherence and Cohesion       | 5.0 | `01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5 (organisation evident but not wholly logical; lack of overall progression; reference inaccurate) |
| Lexical Resource             | 6.0 | `01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 6 (generally adequate; restricted range; lacks precision in word choice) |
| Grammatical Range & Accuracy | 5.0 | `01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5 (complex sentences attempted but faulty; simple sentences most accurate) |
| **Overall**                  | 5.5 | (5+5+6+5)/4 = 5.25 → 5.5 per `01-band-descriptors/scoring-rules.md` §3 |

## What changed from prior feedback
The prior single-shot feedback was substantively right on the band profile and on the structural diagnosis (truncation, drifting body 1, missing conclusion). I keep the band scores and most of the rewrites but sharpen the calibration: this essay sits **below** the band-6 anchor `04-task2-essays/samples-band-6_0.md` §Sample 1 (Wages of Entertainers, ~290 words **with** a real conclusion) because the response here lacks a conclusion and Body 1 drifts to communication history rather than the mathematics→computing causal chain. I also add: (i) a concession-pattern rewrite that lets the candidate take a defensible position on the "most important driver" claim, (ii) a flag on the L1-Korean "not to mention + clause" pattern from `09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md`, and (iii) a cleft-sentence upgrade for the binary topic sentence drawn from `09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md` §5.

## Structural feedback
The single biggest issue is that the essay **has no conclusion** — it ends mid-explanation on "*The graphic on the screen is just a number as well*". For a Task 2 prompt that asks the candidate to take a position on whether mathematics has been *the most important* driver of the computer revolution, the response also never commits to a stance: the thesis is a flat "*I will discuss how mathematics influenced computer science*". Body 1 then loses the math angle entirely and becomes a history of communication tools (Mesopotamia → Egypt → Morse → computers); the only sentence that names a mathematical mechanism is the parenthetical "*the Babylonians used base-60 for timekeeping*". Body 2 has the strongest material — switches, binary, letters-as-numbers — but is only ~70 words and gives no example or implication. Per `05-band-6-to-7/paragraph-structure-band7.md`, the highest-impact fix is **not** the lexical upgrades below; it is rewriting Body 1 around a mathematical mechanism (formal logic / Boolean algebra → switching circuits) and adding a synthesising conclusion that re-states the position. Without that structural change, even with every sentence-level rewrite applied, the response cannot exceed Band 6.

## Focus areas
- **Take a clear position on the "most important driver" claim** — Band 7 TR requires a *clear and developed position throughout*, not a neutral "I will discuss". Drill: `05-band-6-to-7/task-response-upgrades.md`, `09-instructor-tips/06-task2-sentence-frames.md` §2.
- **PEEL development with a maths→computing causal chain** — your Body 1 narrates dates instead of explaining mechanism (Boolean logic → switching circuits → binary). Drill: `05-band-6-to-7/paragraph-structure-band7.md`, `10-task2-topic-banks/topic-03-technology.md`.
- **Concession patterns to defend a strong claim** — to argue maths is *the* driver you must briefly concede engineering/physics also mattered; this is a free TR + GRA upgrade. Drill: `09-instructor-tips/06-task2-sentence-frames.md` §6 (`Important as X is, …`).
- **Articles + countability on abstract/uncountable nouns** — "*new logics*", "*a single hardware*", "*the first ever information*", "*people or dove*" are all article/countability slips that Band 7 cannot tolerate. Drill: `09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md` §A–B, `08-vocabulary-grammar/01-grammar-error-correction-bank.md`.
- **Cohesion beyond demonstrative "that"** — your "*that shone*", "*That strong will*", "*By doing that*", "*The reason it matters*" all hinge on a vague *that/it*. Drill: `05-band-6-to-7/cohesion-upgrades.md` §3 (lexical chains), `09-instructor-tips/08-cohesion-upgrades-instructor.md`.
- **One band-7 grammar showcase per essay (cleft / inversion)** — currently zero. Adding one cleft for the binary claim is the cheapest GRA uplift. Drill: `09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md` §5–6.

## What's working
The technical specifics are genuinely a strength and should stay: "*base-60 for timekeeping*", "*Morse code in 1844*", "*binary digits*", and especially the chain "*every letter is actually a number, and every emoji is also a number. The graphic on the screen is just a number as well*" — that is a real Band-7-shaped insight, just unfinished. Keep this kind of concrete detail and historical anchoring; the next essay should expand each instance into a PEEL paragraph rather than dropping it as a one-liner.

## Per-criterion justification
**Task Response (5.0).** The position never commits: the thesis is "*I will discuss how mathematics influenced computer science*", which is a discussion frame, not a claim about whether maths was *the most important* driver — the descriptor at Band 6 already requires a position "directly relevant to the prompt". Body 1 is largely off-topic (communication history, "*Humans have always wanted to preserve their thoughts somewhere*"), Body 2 stops before developing, and there is no conclusion. Per `01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5, "main parts incompletely addressed" and "ideas not sufficiently developed" both apply.

**Coherence and Cohesion (5.0).** Three paragraphs are visible, but progression breaks down within Body 1, which jumps from Mesopotamia to "*People wanted to deliver their messages only*" to Morse to computers without explicit linking. References are repeatedly unclear: "*Personally, and especially, that shone*" (what is *that*?), "*By doing that, it can calculate*" (what is the antecedent of *that*?). Repetition of "*People wanted ... People wanted ... people finally invented*" is the mechanical pattern flagged in `05-band-6-to-7/cohesion-upgrades.md` §2. The truncation removes any inter-paragraph synthesis.

**Lexical Resource (6.0).** Topic-relevant items are present — "*electronic signals*", "*binary digits*", "*hardware*", "*base-60*", "*switches*". But word-choice slips are dense for one paragraph: "*new logics*" (uncountable in this sense), "*mathematics is inevitable*" (wrong collocation; use *indispensable / essential*), "*the first ever information which only used electronic signals*" (information ≠ message), "*a single hardware*" (uncountable), "*go up to almost countless numbers*" (vague). This is the textbook Band 6 profile in `01-band-descriptors/band-6-vs-7-comparison.md`: meaning is generally clear but range is restricted and precision lapses.

**Grammatical Range & Accuracy (5.0).** Several attempts at complex structures break: "*Computers consist of a full of small switches*" (garbled), "*so that no physical objects such as people or dove needed*" (missing *were*; *dove* uncountable/wrong plural), "*not to mention the Babylonians used base-60*" (clause after *not to mention* — a Korean-L1 transfer pattern noted in `09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md` §E.3). Simple sentences ("*Computers consist of …*", "*The graphic on the screen is just a number as well*") are mostly accurate, but error-free sentences are not frequent — the gating Band 7 criterion in `01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 7.

## Top concrete rewrites (5–10, ranked by band uplift)
| # | Original phrase from essay | Improved (band-7 target) | Reason | Corpus citation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | "In this essay, I will discuss how mathematics influenced computer science." | "Important as engineering and physics have been to computing, this essay will argue that mathematics — through formal logic, binary representation and algorithms — has been the single most decisive driver of the computer revolution." | Adds a clear position, a concession, and a three-point roadmap; one `Adj + as + S + V` showcase. | `09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md` §4; `09-instructor-tips/06-task2-sentence-frames.md` §2 |
| 2 | (essay ends at "…just a number as well." with no conclusion) | "Taken together, these examples show that mathematics provided the very language in which information is stored, transmitted and processed; without it, the hardware breakthroughs of the twentieth century would have remained inert engineering." | Supplies the missing synthesis conclusion and re-states position — the single biggest TR lift. | `05-band-6-to-7/paragraph-structure-band7.md` §5 |
| 3 | "People have built up mathematical rules by defining new logics to solve problems." | "Over millennia, mathematicians have built systems of formal logic that allow problems to be defined precisely and resolved step by step." | Fixes "*new logics*" (uncountable misuse) and names the mechanism (formal logic) the rest of the essay actually depends on. | `08-vocabulary-grammar/02-vocabulary-error-correction-bank.md`; `09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md` §B.2 |
| 4 | "Computers consist of a full of small switches which can turn on and off." | "What underpins every modern computer is, in essence, an array of tiny switches that can be either on or off." | Replaces broken "*a full of*" with a cleft topic sentence — one band-7 GRA showcase in the strongest paragraph. | `09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md` §6 (pseudo-cleft) |
| 5 | "so that no physical objects such as people or dove needed" | "so that messages no longer had to be carried by people or carrier pigeons" | Fixes missing passive *were*, countability of *dove*, and word choice. | `08-vocabulary-grammar/01-grammar-error-correction-bank.md`; `05-band-6-to-7/grammar-upgrades.md` §6 |
| 6 | "If it has billions and trillions of switches, then it can go up to almost countless numbers." | "Were a machine to contain billions of such switches, it could represent numbers and data of essentially unbounded scale." | Replaces vague causality with an inverted second conditional — high-yield GRA showcase. | `05-band-6-to-7/grammar-upgrades.md` §3.1; `09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md` §9 |
| 7 | "the first ever information which only used electronic signals, so that no physical objects such as people or dove needed" | "one of the earliest systems for transmitting information by electrical signal alone, dispensing with physical couriers altogether" | Corrects "*first ever information*" (information ≠ message) and replaces a relative-clause stack with a participle clause. | `05-band-6-to-7/grammar-upgrades.md` §4; `08-vocabulary-grammar/02-vocabulary-error-correction-bank.md` |
| 8 | "mathematics is inevitable" | "mathematical reasoning is indispensable" | Fixes a wrong-collocation (*inevitable* describes events, not tools). | `05-band-6-to-7/vocabulary-upgrades.md` §2; `08-vocabulary-grammar/06-paraphrase-quick-lookup.md` |
| 9 | "not to mention the Babylonians used base-60 for timekeeping" | "the Babylonians, for instance, employed a base-60 numeral system that still shapes how we keep time today" | Removes the L1-Korean "*not to mention + clause*" calque and integrates the example into the argument. | `09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md` §E.3 |
| 10 | "a machine which can do several things in a single hardware, a computer." | "a single piece of hardware capable of performing many different operations — what we now call a computer." | Fixes the uncountable error "*a single hardware*" and tightens the apposition. | `09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md` §B.2; `05-band-6-to-7/band-6-common-errors.md` §A1 |

## Overall band rationale
The four criterion scores average 5.25, which rounds up to **5.5** under `01-band-descriptors/scoring-rules.md` §3. Lexical resource alone reaches Band 6; truncation, position drift, and broken complex sentences hold the other three criteria at Band 5.

## CONVERGENCE: REFINING
The prior feedback chain shown to me is empty, so per the harness rules this must be an early-iteration `REFINING`. There is also still substantive room to push: I added a concession-pattern thesis, an L1-Korean "*not to mention*" diagnosis, and two fresh band-7 grammar showcases (cleft, inverted conditional) that the prior did not contain.
